# evalla

> Safe math expression evaluator with variables, dependencies, and decimal precision. Follows an "Algebra, not code" philosophy.

evalla evaluates mathematical expressions with variable references, topological dependency ordering, and arbitrary-precision arithmetic via decimal.js. It uses a PEG grammar (Peggy) for safe parsing — no eval() or Function().

## Links

- [Playground](https://danmarshall.github.io/evalla/playground): Interactive demo for testing evalla expressions in the browser
- [README](https://danmarshall.github.io/evalla/readme): Full documentation including API reference, examples, and namespace reference
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/evalla): Install via `npm install evalla`
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/danmarshall/evalla): Source code

## Features

- Decimal precision arithmetic (decimal.js) — 0.1 + 0.2 = 0.3 exactly
- Variable dependencies with automatic topological ordering
- Circular dependency detection
- Boolean output from comparisons and logical operators
- Null output support
- Ternary expressions
- Built-in namespaces: $math (trig, log, constants), $unit (length conversions), $angle (deg/rad)
- Object and array input via `value` property with dot-notation and bracket access
- Keywords (return, if, for, etc.) are valid variable names
- Structured error types: ValidationError, ParseError, EvaluationError, SecurityError, CircularDependencyError
- formatResults() utility for rounding output to a fixed number of decimal places

## Security

- No eval() or Function() — AST-based evaluation only
- Blocked access to prototype, __proto__, constructor, and __ prefixed properties
- No function aliasing (namespace functions must be called, not assigned)
- Sandboxed scope via Object.create(null)

## API

```typescript
import { evalla, formatResults } from 'evalla';

const result = await evalla([
  { name: 'a', expr: 'c + 5' },
  { name: 'b', expr: 'a * 2' },
  { name: 'c', expr: '1 + 1' }
]);

// result.values.a === Decimal(7)
// result.values.b === Decimal(14)
// result.order === ['c', 'a', 'b']
```

Input: `ExpressionInput[]` — each item has `name` (string) and either `expr` (math expression string) or `value` (any, for objects/arrays).

Output: `{ values: Record<string, Decimal | boolean | null>, order: string[] }`
